description: Windows Portable Executable format. The PE format is the standard executable file format on Windows, consisting of a DOS MZ stub header followed by a PE signature, COFF file header, and Optional Header with data directories and section tables.
author: WPFHexaEditor Team

functions.parsePEOptionalHeader(var:peHeaderOffset: Parse PE Optional Header including data directories and subsystem info
functions.formatFileSize(var:sizeOfCode): Format the code section size as human-readable string
functions.computeFromVariables(headerParagraphs *: Calculate DOS header size in bytes from paragraph count

blocks.name: DOS MZ Signature
blocks.description: MZ magic bytes (0x5A4D LE) identifying a DOS executable stub

blocks.name: Bytes on Last Page
blocks.description: Number of bytes used on the last 512-byte page of the DOS executable (0 means entire page is used)

blocks.name: Pages in File
blocks.description: Total number of 512-byte pages in the DOS executable including the last partial page

blocks.name: Relocations
blocks.description: Number of relocation entries in the DOS relocation table

blocks.name: Header Size in Paragraphs
blocks.description: Size of the DOS header in 16-byte paragraphs (multiply by 16 for byte size)

blocks.name: Min Extra Paragraphs
blocks.description: Minimum number of extra 16-byte paragraphs of memory needed beyond the end of the program

blocks.name: Max Extra Paragraphs
blocks.description: Maximum number of extra 16-byte paragraphs of memory needed (0xFFFF = as much as available)

blocks.name: Initial SS
blocks.description: Initial stack segment value relative to the start of the loaded program

blocks.name: Initial SP
blocks.description: Initial stack pointer value set when the DOS program begins execution

blocks.name: DOS Checksum
blocks.description: 16-bit checksum of the DOS header (rarely verified, usually 0)

blocks.name: Initial IP
blocks.description: Initial instruction pointer value for the DOS stub entry point

blocks.name: Initial CS
blocks.description: Initial code segment value relative to the start of the loaded program

blocks.name: Reloc Table Offset
blocks.description: File offset of the DOS relocation table (0x40 or greater for PE files)

blocks.name: Overlay Number
blocks.description: Overlay number for DOS overlay management (0 for the main executable)

blocks.name: PE Header Offset (e_lfanew)
blocks.description: File offset to the PE signature (e_lfanew). Typical values are 0x80-0x100. This pointer bridges the DOS stub to the PE header.

blocks.name: PE Signature
blocks.description: PE\0\0 signature (0x00004550 LE) confirming this is a valid Portable Executable

blocks.name: COFF Machine
blocks.description: Target machine architecture for this executable image

blocks.name: Number of Sections
blocks.description: Number of sections in the section table. Windows loader limits this to 96.

blocks.name: TimeDateStamp
blocks.description: Unix epoch timestamp indicating when the image was created by the linker

blocks.name: Pointer to Symbol Table
blocks.description: File offset of the COFF symbol table (0 for PE images, used in COFF object files)

blocks.name: Number of Symbols
blocks.description: Number of entries in the COFF symbol table (0 for PE images)

blocks.name: Size of Optional Header
blocks.description: Size of the Optional Header in bytes. Required for PE executables (typically 0xE0 for PE32, 0xF0 for PE32+).

blocks.name: Characteristics
blocks.description: Flags indicating attributes of the PE file such as whether it is a DLL, executable, or supports large addresses

bitfields.name: RELOCS_STRIPPED
bitfields.description: Relocation information was stripped from the file

bitfields.name: EXECUTABLE_IMAGE
bitfields.description: File is a valid executable image (no unresolved external references)

bitfields.name: LARGE_ADDRESS_AWARE
bitfields.description: Application can handle addresses larger than 2 GB

bitfields.name: 32BIT_MACHINE
bitfields.description: Machine is based on a 32-bit word architecture

bitfields.name: DEBUG_STRIPPED
bitfields.description: Debugging information has been removed from the image file

bitfields.name: DLL
bitfields.description: Image is a Dynamic Link Library (DLL) rather than an executable program

blocks.name: Optional Header Magic
blocks.description: Magic number identifying the PE format variant: PE32 (32-bit) or PE32+ (64-bit)

blocks.name: Major Linker Version
blocks.description: Major version number of the linker that produced the image

blocks.name: Minor Linker Version
blocks.description: Minor version number of the linker that produced the image

blocks.name: Size of Code
blocks.description: Total size of all code sections (.text) in bytes, rounded up to a multiple of FileAlignment

blocks.name: Size of Initialized Data
blocks.description: Total size of all initialized data sections (.data, .rdata) in bytes

blocks.name: Address of Entry Point
blocks.description: Relative Virtual Address (RVA) of the entry point function. For executables this is the start address; for DLLs this is DllMain.

blocks.name: DOS Header Size (bytes)
blocks.description: Calculated size of the DOS header in bytes from the paragraph count (paragraphs x 16)

blocks.name: Is DLL Flag
blocks.description: Determines if the PE file is a DLL by checking bit 13 of the COFF Characteristics flags

blocks.name: DLL Detection
blocks.trueLabel: This file is a Dynamic Link Library (DLL)
blocks.falseLabel: This file is a standard executable (EXE)
blocks.description: Checks the DLL flag (bit 13) in COFF Characteristics to determine if the image is a DLL or EXE

blocks.name: PE Format Detection
blocks.description: Checks the Optional Header magic number to determine if this is a 32-bit (PE32, 0x10B) or 64-bit (PE32+, 0x20B) image

blocks.name: Machine Type
blocks.description: Target CPU architecture for the PE image (e.g., x86, x64, ARM64)

blocks.name: Number of Sections
blocks.description: Total number of sections defined in the PE section table

blocks.name: Is DLL
blocks.description: true if the PE image is a DLL, false if it is an executable

blocks.name: Is 64-bit
blocks.description: true if PE32+ (64-bit image), false if PE32 (32-bit image)

blocks.name: Entry Point (RVA)
blocks.description: Relative Virtual Address of the program entry point

blocks.name: TimeDateStamp
blocks.description: Unix epoch timestamp when the PE image was linked

TechnicalDetails.VariableOffsetFields: PE/COFF/Optional headers are located at the offset specified by e_lfanew (offset 60)
